Re cross compiling: note that [gh-27330](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27330) added a CI job already that does that (without OpenBLAS), it seems to work fine.

I went ahead and tried running the test suite locally on an arm64 machine using the cross-compiled wheel from the workflow added by gh-27330 and everything passed except for 2 failures:

```
=============================================== short test summary info ===============================================
FAILED numpy/_core/tests/test_umath.py::TestComplexFunctions::test_branch_cuts_complex64 - AssertionError: (array([-1.3112233-0.23887786j,  1.3112233+0.23887786j], dtype=complex64), array([-1.3112233-0.2388...
FAILED numpy/_core/tests/test_umath.py::TestComplexFunctions::test_loss_of_precision[complex64] - AssertionError: (np.int64(198), np.float32(0.0008195546), np.float32(3.5762787e-07), 'arcsin')
=========== 2 failed, 45298 passed, 1255 skipped, 28 xfailed, 5 xpassed, 238 warnings in 488.52s (0:08:08) ============
```

Both failures seem to involve issues with inverse trigonometric functions of `np.complex64` type numbers. 

The first test, `test_branch_cuts_complex64`, fails on [this line](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/c045306a188f19d7f919512638bd5f02724c9c7e/numpy/_core/tests/test_umath.py#L4276) and stems from `np.arctan` giving a wrong the numbers 0-2i and 0+2i when they are represented as `np.complex64` types. This can be reproduced by just running `np.arctan(2j)` with and without converting it to `np.complex64` first. I also tried other complex values and they all seem to give the wrong value when they are `np.complex64` types but give the correct value when they are `complex` types:

```
>>> import numpy as np
>>>
>>> x1 = 2j
>>> np.arctan(x1)
np.complex128(1.5707963267948966+0.5493061443340549j)
>>> np.arctan(np.complex64(x1))
np.complex64(1.3112233+0.23887786j)
>>>
>>> x2 = -2j
>>> np.arctan(x2)
np.complex128(-1.5707963267948966-0.5493061443340549j)
>>> np.arctan(np.complex64(x2))
np.complex64(-1.3112233-0.23887786j)
>>>
>>> x3 = 1.2j
>>> np.arctan(x3)
np.complex128(1.5707963267948966+1.1989476363991853j)
>>> np.arctan(np.complex64(x3))
np.complex64(1.1176459+0.36133197j)
>>>
>>> x4 = 2-1.2j
>>> np.arctan(x4)
np.complex128(1.2041400197032617-0.19576054616912333j)
>>> np.arctan(np.complex64(x4))
np.complex64(-1.1176459-0.36133197j)
>>>
>>> x5 = 2+0j
>>> np.arctan(x5)
np.complex128(1.1071487177940904+0j)
>>> np.arctan(np.complex64(x5))
np.complex64(0j)
```

For the second test, `test_loss_of_precision[complex64]`, fails on [this line](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/c045306a188f19d7f919512638bd5f02724c9c7e/numpy/_core/tests/test_umath.py#L4373) and can be reproduced by rerunning the imaginary z arcsin and arctan sections of the `check` function:

```
>>> import numpy as np
>>>
>>> x = np.logspace(-20, -3.001, 200)
>>> rtol = 2.1*np.finfo(np.complex64).eps
>>> x = x.astype(np.complex64(0.).real.dtype)
>>> z = (1j*x).astype(np.complex64)
>>> d = np.absolute(np.arcsinh(x)/np.arcsin(z).imag - 1)
>>>
>>> rtol
np.float32(2.503395e-07)
>>>
>>> d[-3:] < rtol
array([ True, False, False])
>>> x[-2:]
array([0.00081955, 0.0009977 ], dtype=float32)
>>> z[-2:]
array([0.+0.00081955j, 0.+0.0009977j ], dtype=complex64)
>>> d[-2:]
array([3.5762787e-07, 3.5762787e-07], dtype=float32)
>>>
>>> d = np.absolute(np.arctanh(x)/np.arctan(z).imag - 1)
>>>
>>> d[-5:] < rtol
array([ True, False, False, False, False])
>>> d[-4:]
array([3.5762787e-07, 5.9604645e-07, 7.1525574e-07, 1.0728836e-06],
      dtype=float32)
```

### Issue

Running `conda smithy rerender` in a feedstock with the `self_hosted: true` option under `github_actions` seems to get read a false. Once it generates the workflow yaml file it sets the `runs-on` option as `runs_on: ['windows-latest']`. instead of the expected `self-hosted`. 

I ran conda smithy with the following `conda-forge.yml`:
```
build_platform: {osx_arm64: osx_64, win_arm64: win_arm64}
conda_forge_output_validation: true
provider: {linux_aarch64: default, linux_ppc64le: default}
github:
  branch_name: main
  tooling_branch_name: main
github_actions:
  self_hosted: true
  triggers:
    - pull_request
    - push
provider:
  win_arm64: github_actions
conda_build:
  pkg_format: '2'
test: native_and_emulated
```

Which resulted in the following `.github/workflows/conda-build.yml`:
```

Fixes an inverted conditional when the `self-hosted` option is set to `true`. It now correctly generates the github actions workflow.

Tested locally with the following conda-forge.yml:
```
build_platform: {osx_arm64: osx_64, win_arm64: win_arm64}
conda_forge_output_validation: true
provider: {linux_aarch64: default, linux_ppc64le: default}
github:
  branch_name: main
  tooling_branch_name: main
github_actions:
  self_hosted: true
  triggers:
    - pull_request
    - push
provider:
  win_arm64: github_actions
conda_build:
  pkg_format: '2'
test: native_and_emulated
```

Fixes an inverted conditional when the `self-hosted` option is set to `true`. It now correctly generates the github actions workflow.

Tested locally with the following conda-forge.yml:
```
build_platform: {osx_arm64: osx_64, win_arm64: win_arm64}
conda_forge_output_validation: true
provider: {linux_aarch64: default, linux_ppc64le: default}
github:
  branch_name: main
  tooling_branch_name: main
github_actions:
  self_hosted: true
  triggers:
    - pull_request
    - push
provider:
  win_arm64: github_actions
conda_build:
  pkg_format: '2'
test: native_and_emulated
```

Before this fix the runs-on option for arm64 windows was `runs_on: ['windows-latest']` and after this fix it is `runs_on: ['windows', 'ARM64', Fixes an inverted conditional when the `self-hosted` option is set to `true`. It now correctly generates the github actions workflow.

Tested locally with the following conda-forge.yml:
```
build_platform: {osx_arm64: osx_64, win_arm64: win_arm64}
conda_forge_output_validation: true
provider: {linux_aarch64: default, linux_ppc64le: default}
github:
  branch_name: main
  tooling_branch_name: main
github_actions:
  self_hosted: true
  triggers:
    - pull_request
    - push
provider:
  win_arm64: github_actions
conda_build:
  pkg_format: '2'
test: native_and_emulated
```

Tested locally with the following conda-forge.yml:
```
build_platform: {osx_arm64: osx_64, win_arm64: win_arm64}
conda_forge_output_validation: true
provider: {linux_aarch64: default, linux_ppc64le: default}
github:
  branch_name: main
  tooling_branch_name: main
github_actions:
  self_hosted: true
  triggers:
    - pull_request
    - push
provider:
  win_arm64: github_actions
conda_build:
  pkg_format: '2'
test: native_and_emulated
```
